back_translation: Now the lot for the tribe of the sons of Judah according to their clans was at the border of Edom, the Wilderness of Zin to the south, at the edge of Teman (the south).
Their southern border was from the edge of the Salt Sea, from the tongue (bay) that turns to the south.
It went out to the south of the Ascent of Akrabbim, passed over to Zin, went up south of Kadesh-barnea, passed through Hezron, went up to Addar, and turned around to Karka.
It passed to Azmon and went out to the Wadi of Egypt, and the boundary's outgoings were to the sea. This shall be your southern border.
The eastern border was the Salt Sea as far as the end of the Jordan. The border on the northern side started from the tongue of the sea, from the end of the Jordan.
The border went up to Beth-hoglah and passed north of Beth-arabah, and the border went up to the Stone of Bohan son of Reuben.

Then the border went up to Debir from the Valley of Achor, and northward turning to Gilgal which faces the Ascent of Adummim south of the wadi; and the border passed to the waters of En-shemesh, and its outgoings were to En-rogel.

Then the border went up to the Valley of Ben-hinnom along the southern slope of the Jebusite (that is, Jerusalem), and the border went up to the top of the hill which faces the Valley of Hinnom to the west, at the northern end of the Valley of Rephaim.
From the hilltop the border curved to the spring of the Waters of Nephtoah and went out to the cities of Mount Ephron; and the border curved to Baalah (that is, Kiriath-jearim).
The border curled from Baalah westward to Mount Seir, and passed to the northern slope of Mount Jearim (that is, Chesalon), and went down to Beth-shemesh and passed to Timnah.
The border went out to the northern slope of Ekron, and the border curved to Shikkeron, passed Mount Baalah, and went out to Jabneel; and the outgoings of the border were to the sea.
The western border was the Great Sea and the boundary. This is the border around the sons of Judah according to their clans.
And to Caleb son of Jephunneh he gave a portion among the sons of Judah, according to the command of the LORD to Joshua — Kiriath-arba (the father of Anak), that is, Hebron.
And Caleb dispossessed from there the three sons of Anak — Sheshai and Ahiman and Talmai, the descendants of Anak.
He went up from there to the inhabitants of Debir; and the name of Debir formerly was Kiriath-sepher.
And Caleb said, 'Whoever strikes Kiriath-sepher and captures it, I will give him Acsah my daughter as a wife.'
And Othniel son of Kenaz, brother of Caleb, captured it; and he gave him Acsah his daughter as a wife.
And it happened when she came, she urged him to ask from her father a field. She dismounted from the donkey, and Caleb said to her, 'What is the matter with you?'
She said, 'Give me a blessing, for the land of the Negev you have given me; give me also springs of water.' And he gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.
This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Judah according to their clans.
These were the cities from the edge for the tribe of the sons of Judah to the border of Edom in the south: Kabzeel and Eder and Jagur,
and Kinah and Dimonah and Adadah,
and Kedesh and Hazor and Ithnan,
Ziph and Telem and Bealoth,
and Hazor-hadattah and Kerioth-hezron, that is, Hazor,
Amam and Shema and Moladah,
and Hazar-gaddah and Heshmon and Beth-pelet,
and Hazar-shual and Beersheba and Biziothiah,
Baalah and Iim and Ezem,
and Eltolad and Chesil and Hormah,
and Ziklag and Madmannah and Sansannah,
and Lebaoth and Shilhim and Ain and Rimmon. In all, twenty-nine cities with their villages.
In the lowland: Eshtaol and Zorah and Ashnah,
and Zanoah and En-gannim, Tappuah and Enam,
Jarmuth and Adullam, Socoh and Azekah,
and Shaaraim and Adithaim, and Gederah and Gederothaim — fourteen cities with their villages.
Zenan and Hadashah and Migdal-gad,
and Dilan and Mizpeh and Joktheel,
Lachish and Bozkath and Eglon,
and Cabbon and Lahmas and Chitlish,
and Gederoth, Beth-dagon and Naamah and Makkedah — sixteen cities with their villages.
Libnah and Ether and Ashan,
and Iphtah and Ashnah and Nezib,
and Keilah and Achzib and Mareshah — nine cities with their villages.
Ekron with its daughter-cities and its villages.
From Ekron and to the west, everything that is on the side of Ashdod and their villages.
Ashdod with its daughter-cities and its villages, Gaza with its daughter-cities and its villages, as far as the Wadi of Egypt and the Great Sea and the boundary.
In the hill country: Shamir and Jattir and Socoh,
and Dannah and Kiriath-sannah, that is, Debir,
and Anab and Eshtemoh and Anim,
and Goshen and Holon and Giloh — eleven cities with their villages.
Arab and Dumah and Eshan,
and Janim and Beth-tappuah and Aphekah,
and Humtah and Kiriath-arba, that is, Hebron, and Zior — nine cities with their villages.
Maon, Carmel and Ziph and Juttah,
and Jezreel and Jokdeam and Zanoah,
Kain, Gibeah, and Timnah — ten cities with their villages.
Halhul, Beth-zur and Gedor,
and Maarath and Beth-anoth and Eltekon — six cities with their villages.
Kiriath-baal, that is, Kiriath-jearim, and Rabbah — two cities with their villages.
In the wilderness: Beth-arabah, Middin and Secacah,
and Nibshan and the City of Salt and En-gedi — six cities with their villages.
But the Jebusites, the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the sons of Judah could not dispossess; and the Jebusites have dwelt with the sons of Judah in Jerusalem to this day.
